title: Now Close The Windows
content:
Now close the windows and hush all the fields:
If the trees must, let them silently toss;
No bird is singing now, and if there is,
Be it my loss.

It will be long ere the marshes resume,
I will be long ere the earliest bird:
So close the windows and not hear the wind,
But see all wind-stirred.
meaning:
Robert Lee Frost's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Now Close The Windows"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
